front.title: Human-AI Relationships in Science Fiction
front.subtitle: Exploring the evolving bond between humans and artificial intelligence.

back.text:
Science fiction has long imagined the relationship between humans and artificial intelligence (AI), often reflecting our hopes and fears. From early works like Karel Čapek's 1920 play 'R.U.R.' (Rossum's Universal Robots), which introduced the term 'robot,' to Isaac Asimov's 'I, Robot' series, these narratives explore the complexities of coexistence with machines. They raise ethical questions about autonomy, consciousness, and the potential for AI to surpass human intelligence.

As technology advances, the themes in science fiction become increasingly relevant. Today, AI is integrated into daily life, influencing everything from healthcare to entertainment. These stories not only entertain but also serve as cautionary tales, prompting discussions about the moral implications of creating sentient beings. By examining these fictional relationships, we gain insight into our own interactions with technology and the future we are shaping together with AI.
